gauge.name: Wolf River at Collierville

impacts.statement: Collierville-Arlington Road is under 4 feet of water south of the bridge. Most houses east of Collierville-Arlington Road between the east end of Shelton Road and Arlington Hall Drive are flooded. Houses northeast of Alexandria Drive by the interstate highway are flooded. Many houses on the north end of Aston Court Subdivision between Jennifer Lane and Johnash Court are flooded. Johnson Park is all flooded.
Collierville-Arlington Road is under 3 feet of water south of the bridge. Road to school buildings east of Houston Levee Road is flooded.

Collierville-Arlington Road is under over 2 feet of water south of the bridge. The south end of the parking lot of the church just north of the river at Houston Levee Road is flooded. The parking lot of the school east of Houston Levee Road begins to flood.
Up to one and half feet of water is rushing over Collierville-Arlington Road just north of Shelton Road East.

Four to six inches of water is flowing over the Collierville-Arlington Road just north of Shelton Road East. At Johnson Park, baseball fields are flooding, and the road to the soccer fields is closed. There is extensive flooding of the school east of Houston Levee Road.
Much of the Collierville Greenbelt is flooded.
Campus of school east of Houston Levee Road begins to flood. Northwest part of Johnson Park is flooding.
The Wolf begins to go over its banks.
Water is backing into small streams that flow into the Wolf River, and old stream beds in the swampy bottom are beginning to fill with water again.

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.

gauge.forecastReliability: Graphical forecasts are not available during times of high water, forecast crest information can be found in the local WFO web page.
